reason: The statement applies because the text describes David as a righteous man whom the LORD (Yahweh) protects while punishing the wicked. In the text, it is evident that David is chosen and favored by the LORD and is successful in battles against the enemies of Israel, such as the Philistines. Additionally, the text shows David's reverence and devotion to the LORD through his actions and offerings, highlighting his righteousness in the eyes of the LORD.
